title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-20
content:
NAV: $4,315.88  |  Holdings: $3,813.44  |  Cash: $502.44  Week return: +2.13%  vs S&P 500: -2.41%
Market Context
S&P 500 gained +4.54% and Nasdaq +6.84% for the week of April 14-17, 2026, closing at all-time highs (S&P 7,041+). Nasdaq posted its 12th consecutive winning session — longest streak since 2009. CRITICAL NEW DEVELOPMENT: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz as of approximately April 18-19, citing US breaches of trust — reversing the April 17 reopening that drove last week's equity rally. This reintroduces geopolitical risk premiums and materially strengthens the GLD thesis. AVGO surged +28% on AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031). Dow gained +1.79% on Friday April 17.
Fed held at 3.50-3.75% for second consecutive meeting; FOMC April 28-29 widely expected to hold — JPMorgan now sees NO cuts in 2026 and a potential 25bps hike in Q3 2027 if inflation persists. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM; core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12. Tariff passthrough expected to peak in Q2 2026 — rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. Iran Hormuz re-closure is the dominant new macro development: oil supply risk restored, gold safe-haven bid reasserting, inflation expectations re-elevated. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21-34% YTD, Materials, Industrials leading over Technology — April saw counter-trend tech rally which may reverse with Hormuz re-closure.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,315.88. Week return +2.13% vs S&P 500 +4.54% = underperformance of -2.41pp, entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (26.2% actual vs ~36% target). CSPX ADD recommended for NINTH consecutive report without execution — singular operational failure of this portfolio. YTD since April 4 seed ($4,191): +2.98% vs CSPX YTD +3.16% — now marginally behind benchmark. All 7 positions positive unrealized. Iran Hormuz re-closure STRENGTHENS GLD thesis and partially restores U03A capital preservation value. ASML China mix fell to 19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold — WATCH flag). MA earnings date corrected to April 30 (not April 23). WHT YTD: only $3.33 from MA dividend.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single priority action: ADD $250 to CSPX at April 20 Monday open — unconditional, 9th consecutive recommendation. Post-trade: CSPX rises from 26.2% to ~31.5% of NAV; cash drops from 11.6% to ~5.8%. Subsequent steps: (1) conditional +$100 MA on April 30 earnings beat; (2) U03A stage-down -$100 post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish signal received; (3) unconditional CSPX top-up by May 3 hard deadline with remaining cash. ASML monitored but not trimmed at current size due to commission inefficiency. Decisions driven purely by current thesis strength and market conditions — not anchored to prior report weights.
Cash Strategy
$502.44 available (11.6% of NAV) — structural drag, not dry powder. STEP 1 (April 20, unconditional): Deploy $250 into CSPX; post-trade cash ~$252. STEP 2 (April 30, conditional): $100 into MA on earnings beat; post-trade cash ~$152. STEP 3 (May 3 hard deadline, unconditional): Deploy ~$102-$152 into CSPX; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3. Iran Hormuz re-closure does not override the deployment schedule.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 when NAV > $5,000): Broadcom surged +28% in April 2026 on landmark AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031) — highest-conviction new position candidate. Custom silicon leadership, VMware networking dominance, $438-480 analyst price targets. Zero effective dividend = 0% WHT. Complements NVDA (custom silicon vs standard GPUs). Gate: NAV > $5,000 AND CSPX gap fully closed. Current NAV $4,315 is below threshold. Post-surge entry at ~$393 is less attractive than original identification; wait for NAV growth or 10-15% AVGO pullback to $340-355 range.
Action Items
CRITICAL — Monday April 20 open (TODAY): Buy CSPX $250 (~0.326 shares at ~$766.84). NINTH consecutive report. Non-negotiable — no further deferral. Hormuz re-closure may cause brief dip on open; use it as a slightly better entry.
URGENT — April 28-29 FOMC: Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish signal = initiate U03A first stage-down (~$100 redeployed into CSPX, reducing U03A from 13.9% toward 12% of NAV).
IMPORTANT — April 29 AMZN Q1 earnings: Monitor AWS growth rate (>20% target), AI revenue disclosure, operating margin. Beat expected. No pre-earnings action required.
IMPORTANT — April 30 MA Q1 earnings (CORRECTED DATE — not April 23): ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$102-$152 post-CSPX and MA trades) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions permitted.
ONGOING — ASML WHT verification: Confirm 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate on next IBKR tax document. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to ASML.AS (Euronext Amsterdam).
WATCH — ASML China mix: Q2 2026 results are the critical trigger. If China mix <15% of net sales OR DUV restriction bill advances materially in Congress, exit full position and redeploy into CSPX/NVDA.
WATCH — GLD: Iran Hormuz re-closure (April 18-19) REINFORCES HOLD. 4-week risk-on clock has NOT started and is now fully reset. GLD $420 price alert remains active for 25% trim trigger. All four invalidation conditions remain untriggered.
NEW DEVELOPMENT — Iran Hormuz re-closure: Monitor closely. If formal peace deal and durable reopening occur, reassess GLD invalidation timeline and accelerate U03A stage-down. Conversely, if conflict escalates, GLD ADD from U03A redeployment becomes higher priority than CSPX.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-18
content:
NAV: $4,315.88  |  Holdings: $3,813.44  |  Cash: $502.44  Week return: +2.13%  vs S&P 500: -1.16%
Market Context
S&P 500 closed at 7,041.28 on April 17 — first-ever close above 7,000 (+3.29% for the week April 11-17). A further ~1.3% surge estimated on April 18 on Iran Strait of Hormuz reopening news (~7,133 estimated close, ~+4.63% weekly total). Nasdaq +5.2% for the week, 12th consecutive positive session — longest win streak since 2009. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz completely open on April 17; oil fell ~11%. US naval blockade technically remains in force; ceasefire violated by both sides; no formal peace deal yet. AVGO surged +28% in April 2026 on new AI partnership contracts with Google, Meta, and Anthropic including a 3.5GW compute infrastructure deal.
Fed held at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC widely expected to hold (JPMorgan: 'an easy call to stay on hold'). PCE inflation forecast revised UP to 2.7% for 2026. March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since April 2024) driven by Iran war energy costs; gasoline +18.9% YoY. Expected to peak ~3.6-4% headline in April-May then decline toward 3% by year-end as Hormuz reopening reverses energy prices — sets up H2 2026 rate cut window. Core CPI well-contained at +2.6% YoY. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21%, Materials +17%, Staples +15%, Industrials +12% outperforming; this specific week saw Nasdaq lead +5.2% on geopolitical risk resolution. Powell succession uncertainty (term expires May 2026) remains a live policy risk. AVGO +28% in April on AI custom silicon demand surge.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (75/100). NAV $4,315.88, +2.13% week vs S&P 500 +3.29% = underperformance of 1.16pp entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag. The CSPX $250 ADD has now been recommended for EIGHT consecutive reports without execution — this remains the portfolio's singular critical operational failure. All 7 positions are in the green. ASML improved to +4.49% unrealized (up from +0.98% last week at $1,410 vs $1,459.80 now). AMZN at +17.13% is the strongest single-stock position. WHT structure is clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD. YTD since inception (April 4 seed $4,191): +2.98%. Key upcoming catalysts: MA earnings April 23, FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single highest-priority action: ADD $250 into CSPX at Monday April 20 open (8th consecutive recommendation — non-negotiable). Post-trade: CSPX rises from 26.2% to ~31.8% of NAV; cash drops from 11.6% to ~5.8%. Conditional: ADD $100 MA on April 23 earnings beat (cross-border >14%, VAS >20%, guidance maintained). U03A stage-down begins post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish — reduce toward 10% of NAV, redeploy into CSPX. All other positions HOLD. No exits warranted.
Cash Strategy
$502.44 available (11.6% of NAV) — structural drag while S&P 500 is at all-time highs. STEP 1 (Monday April 20): Deploy $250 into CSPX; post-trade cash ~$252. STEP 2 (April 23 conditional): $100 into MA on earnings beat; post-trade cash ~$152. STEP 3 (May 3 hard deadline): Deploy ~$102 remaining into CSPX unconditionally; retain $50 minimum buffer. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3 under any circumstances.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 when NAV > $5,000): Broadcom surged +28% in April 2026 on new AI partnerships with Google, Meta, and Anthropic (3.5GW compute deal). AI custom silicon leadership, $73B total AI backlog, AI chip revenue expected to double in Q1 2026, networking dominance via VMware. Negligible dividend = 0% effective WHT. Highest-conviction new position candidate — do NOT buy now; wait for NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap closed.
META ($200 when NAV > $5,000): Zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI capex, Llama ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet, AI-enabled wearables monetization accelerating. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000.
Action Items
CRITICAL — Monday April 20 open: Buy CSPX $250 (~0.326 shares at ~$766.84). EIGHTH consecutive report. S&P 500 at all-time highs, Hormuz open, risk-on. Non-negotiable — no further deferral.
URGENT — Wednesday April 23: MA Q1 2026 earnings report. ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS >20% AND guidance maintained. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate partial exit if miss or guidance cut.
IMPORTANT — April 28-29: FOMC rate decision. Dovish signal = initiate U03A first stage-down (~$100 redeployed into CSPX, beginning reduction from 13.9% toward 10% of NAV).
IMPORTANT — April 29: AMZN Q1 2026 earnings. Monitor AWS growth rate (must sustain >20%), AI revenue disclosure, operating margin. Beat expected per consensus. Post-beat: revisit sizing if AWS >25% and guidance raised.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$102-$152 post-CSPX and MA trades) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions.
ONGOING: Verify ASML WHT on next IBKR dividend statement — 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA. If 30% confirmed, switch to AMS-listed ASML shares (ASML.AS on Euronext Amsterdam).
WATCH: GLD invalidation timer — formal US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz durably open + 4+ consecutive weeks sustained risk-on before triggering 25% GLD trim into CSPX.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-18
content:
NAV: $4,314.87  |  Holdings: $3,772.46  |  Cash: $542.41  Week return: +2.11%  vs S&P 500: -2.52%
Market Context
S&P 500 hit all-time highs this week, closing at 7,041.28 on April 17 (first-ever close above 7,000 in history) and surging a further ~1.3% on April 18 as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open.' Estimated April 18 close ~7,133. Weekly S&P gain ~+4.63% (from April 11 baseline ~6,817). Nasdaq surged +5.2% and notched its 12th consecutive positive session — longest win streak since 2009. The Hormuz reopening caused oil to drop 12% and equities to extend gains. US naval blockade technically remains until a full formal peace deal; Trump said a deal is expected 'in days.' Portfolio NAV adjusted to $4,314.87 after NVDA 205C call option expired worthless on April 17 ($8.50 total loss accepted).
1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Strait of Hormuz declared 'completely open' by Iran on April 18 — the single most significant geopolitical development of the week. Oil dropped 12%. US blockade remains until formal peace deal but Trump expects agreement within days. Full resolution will trigger GLD invalidation condition review (4+ week sustained risk-on requirement). 2. INFLATION: March 2026 CPI +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM — driven by a 21.2% monthly gasoline surge (largest ever recorded), directly caused by Iran war Hormuz disruption. Core CPI +2.6% YoY. With Hormuz now opening, analysts project CPI peaks near 4% then declines toward 3% by year-end — energy shock reversal is now in motion. 3. FED: Rate held at 3.50-3.75%. FOMC April 28-29 is the next critical catalyst. Dot plot signals one cut in 2026; markets price two (50bps total). Ceasefire and declining energy inflation increase probability of H2 2026 cuts. 4. GOLD: XAU/USD rose to ~$4,878 on April 18 — counterintuitively UP despite Hormuz opening, as lower inflation reduces tightening need (gold positive). Institutional targets surged dramatically: JPM $6,300, UBS $6,200 (mid-2026), Goldman Sachs $5,400. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Broad 2026 YTD trend is value over tech (Energy +21-25% YTD, Materials +18%, Staples +16%). This specific week saw tech lead again with Nasdaq +5.2%. Energy now taking a hit on Hormuz reopening (oil -12%). 6. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated rates (ex-China 125%) continues. Business investment still suppressed by policy uncertainty. US average effective tariff rate in double digits.
Portfolio Health
Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,314.87 (adjusted for expired NVDA 205C option) — up +$88.94 (+2.11%) from April 11 Friday baseline of $4,225.93, and +$123.87 (+2.96%) YTD from April 4 seed of $4,191.00. Weekly return +2.11% vs S&P 500 estimated +4.63% — underperformance of 2.52pp entirely attributable to 12.57% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (25.84% of NAV vs 36% target = -10.16pp gap). The CSPX $250 ADD is now in its SEVENTH consecutive weekly report without execution — this is the portfolio's singular operational failure and it has cost measurable compounding returns as the S&P has surged to all-time highs. NVDA 205C call option confirmed expired worthless April 17 — $8.50 total loss absorbed. WHT structure remains clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD (all from MA dividend). Total unrealized P&L on equity/ETF positions: +$209.22. ASML is the weakest position: barely above cost (+0.98%), facing DUV restriction bill risk and China mix compression. All other equity positions are positive with intact or strengthening theses.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Single clear action drives the entire rebalancing decision: ADD $250 to CSPX immediately (7th consecutive report, non-negotiable). Post-buy: CSPX rises from 25.84% to ~31.6% of NAV; cash falls from 12.57% to ~6.8%. Remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$292): reserve $100 for MA earnings add (April 23 or 30 — verify date); deploy $192 into CSPX by May 3 hard deadline (15 days). U03A stage-down (14% to 10% of NAV) is on track — execute contingent on FOMC April 28-29 guidance and CSPX gap closing. No other positions require rebalancing. ASML is too small to trim economically despite WEAKER flag.
Cash Strategy
$542.41 available (12.57% of NAV) — this is structural drag, not dry powder. Step 1 (Monday April 20): Deploy $250 into CSPX. Post-trade cash $292.41 (6.78% of NAV). Step 2 (April 23 or 30, MA earnings): ADD $100 into MA contingent on earnings beat. Post-trade cash ~$192. Step 3 (May 3 hard deadline — 15 days): Deploy remaining ~$192 into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum operating buffer at all times. Cash must not exceed 10% of NAV past May 3 under any circumstances.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 (when NAV > $5,000)): Broadcom: highest-conviction new-position candidate. AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator programs), networking dominance via VMware integration. Shares up 50%+ YTD in 2026; named by BofA as one of 6 stocks leading the $1T chip surge. Zero effective dividend = 0% WHT. Complements NVDA as second AI semiconductor name. DO NOT BUY NOW — close CSPX gap and deploy Tranche 3 first. Revisit when NAV > $5,000.
META ($200 (when NAV > $5,000)): Meta Platforms: zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI infrastructure capex, Llama open-source ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet. WhatsApp Business and Reality Labs AI monetization accelerating. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap fully closed.
Action Items
CRITICAL IMMEDIATE (Monday April 20 open): Buy CSPX $250 (~0.33 units at ~$756-770). SEVENTH consecutive report. S&P 500 at all-time highs, Hormuz open, risk-on fully restored. No further deferral under any circumstances.
URGENT (today April 18): Verify MA Q1 2026 earnings date — April 23 or April 30? Conflicting sources. Prepare ADD $100 decision for earnings beat scenario. Set calendar alert for the correct date.
April 20 (Monday): Confirm NVDA 205C option shows as expired/closed in IBKR with $8.50 total loss fully absorbed.
April 23 or April 30 — MA earnings: ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% and VAS growth >20% with confident full-year guidance. HOLD if in-line. REDUCE if miss or guidance cut.
April 28-29 — FOMC: Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish signal or explicit rate cut = trigger U03A stage-down from ~14% toward 10% of NAV (redeploy into CSPX).
April 29 — AMZN Q1 earnings: Monitor AWS revenue, operating margin, and AI monetization. Beat expected; no pre-earnings action required.
May 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy all remaining Tranche 3 cash (~$192 post-CSPX-and-MA) into CSPX unconditionally. No extensions.
ONGOING — ASML WHT: Verify on next IBKR tax document whether 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate is applied. If 30% confirmed, initiate switch to AMS-listed ASML shares to eliminate US NRA withholding.
WATCH — GLD: Monitor Iran ceasefire durability. Full US-Iran peace deal + Hormuz formally reopened + 4+ weeks sustained risk-on = trim 25% of GLD position and redeploy into CSPX.
WATCH — ASML: If China mix falls below 15% of net sales in Q2 2026 OR DUV restriction bill advances materially in Congress, initiate partial trim and redeploy into CSPX.

title: Portfolio Advisory — 2026-04-17
content:
NAV: $4,323.37  |  Holdings: $3,780.96  |  Cash: $542.41  Week return: +2.30%  vs S&P 500: -1.00%
Market Context
S&P 500 closed at 7,041.28 on April 16 — a new all-time high and the first close above 7,000 in history. Weekly gain +3.3%; Nasdaq surged +5.2% and notched its 12th consecutive positive session, the longest win streak since 2009. The Iran ceasefire (April 8) is highly fragile: violated by both sides, Iran refuses to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Trump declared a US naval blockade on April 13. Despite this instability, equity markets are pricing in full resolution. March 2026 CPI: +3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM), a large beat driven by 21.2% monthly gasoline price surge tied to Iran war Strait of Hormuz disruption. Technology sector led with +4.82%; Energy fell -4.07% on ceasefire euphoria; Financials +2.50%. Gold (GLD) at $440.08, with XAU/USD ~$4,830/oz — actively supported by Iran war fragility and central bank inflows (920 tonnes ETF holdings, up from 840t end-2025). J.P. Morgan targets XAU $5,055 by Q4 2026.
1. FED: Funds rate held at 3.50-3.75%. Next FOMC April 28-29 — critical near-term catalyst. Market consensus: two rate cuts in 2026, timing constrained by Iran- and tariff-driven inflation. Powell term expires May 2026 — succession uncertainty is a live policy risk. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY, highest in months. Headline projected to peak ~4% by May-June 2026 if Hormuz reopens. Core CPI 3-month annualized ~2.9%. No rate cuts before inflation trajectory becomes clearer. 3. IRAN WAR: Fragile two-week ceasefire (April 8) mediated by Pakistan — violated by both sides. Iran refuses to reopen Strait of Hormuz. US naval blockade active since April 13. Oil supply disruption persisting; energy inflation elevated. 4. TARIFFS: 90-day pause on elevated tariff rates (ex-China at 125%) continues. US average effective tariff rate in double digits. Business investment suppressed by policy uncertainty. 5. GOLD BULLISH CASE STRENGTHENING: XAU ~$4,830/oz. J.P. Morgan $5,055 Q4 target; RBC $4,800 year-end; Wells Fargo $6,100-6,300. Central bank inflows at multi-decade highs. Iran war fragility sustaining safe-haven premium.
Portfolio Health
Grade: B- (78/100). NAV $4,323.37 — up +$97.44 (+2.30%) from April 11 baseline of $4,225.93, and +$132.37 (+3.16%) from April 4 seed of $4,191. Weekly return +2.30% vs S&P 500 +3.30% — underperformance of 1.0pp entirely attributable to 12.55% idle cash drag. The CSPX $250 buy is now in its SIXTH consecutive weekly report without execution — this is the portfolio's primary structural failure. CSPX underweight: 25.79% of total NAV vs 36% target = -10.21pp gap. Total unrealized P&L (equity positions): +$209.22 (+5.85% on cost basis ~$3,571.74). NVDA 205C call option (NVDA 17APR26 205 C) expires worthless TODAY — accept $8.50 total loss. WHT structure clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD. U03A YTM compressed to 3.68% (down from 4.3% thesis baseline). ASML China mix declining (36% to 19% of net sales) but guidance raised.
Position Review
Rebalancing Assessment
Urgently warranted on two fronts. (1) CSPX ADD $250 — sixth consecutive recommendation; post-trade closes CSPX gap from -10.21pp to -4.5pp, reduces cash drag from 12.55% to 6.8%. (2) Tranche 3 ($242.41 post-CSPX) must deploy by May 3 hard deadline (16 days): deploy $200 CSPX unconditionally if no 5-7% S&P pullback; or $142.41 CSPX + $100 MA on pullback. Medium-term: U03A trim from ~14% toward 10% of NAV as Fed rate cuts materialize. No other positions require rebalancing.
Cash Strategy
$542.41 available; $492.41 deployable after $50 buffer. Holding 12.55% idle cash while S&P 500 is at all-time highs is structurally destructive. STEP 1 IMMEDIATE: Deploy $250 into CSPX — cash falls to ~$292.41 (~6.8% of post-trade NAV). STEP 2 TRANCHE 3 (May 3 hard deadline, 16 days): Trigger A — S&P drops 5-7% to ~6,548-6,609: deploy $142.41 CSPX + $100 MA. Trigger B — May 3 hard deadline: deploy $200 CSPX unconditionally, retain ~$42.41 minimum buffer. Cash must not remain above 10% of NAV past May 3.
New Opportunities
AVGO ($300 (when NAV > $5,000 and CSPX at target)): Broadcom: highest-conviction new-position candidate once NAV exceeds $5,000. AI custom silicon leadership (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerator), 24% total revenue growth, 74% AI semiconductor revenue growth, VMware networking integration. Minimal dividend (~1.3%) — WHT impact ~$3-4/year at suggested size, acceptable. Complements NVDA as second AI semiconductor name. DO NOT BUY NOW — close CSPX gap and deploy Tranche 3 first. Revisit after May 3 Tranche 3 deployment with NAV > $5,000.
META ($200 (when NAV > $5,000)): Meta Platforms: zero dividend = 0% WHT. $60B 2026 AI infrastructure capex, Llama open-source ecosystem moat, advertising duopoly with Alphabet, near-term AI monetization more visible than most peers. Research candidate — not actionable until NAV > $5,000 and CSPX gap fully closed.
Action Items
CRITICAL IMMEDIATE: Buy CSPX $250 (~0.331 units at ~$756). SIXTH consecutive report. S&P 500 at new all-time high 7,041.28. No further deferral under any circumstances.
TODAY April 17: NVDA 205C expires worthless — no action required. Accept $8.50 total loss. Do not roll.
APRIL 28-29: Fed FOMC meeting. Watch rate decision and forward guidance. Dovish pivot or rate cut signal = trigger to begin U03A trim from ~14% toward 10% of NAV.
APRIL 30: MA Q1 2026 earnings. Monitor US and cross-border payment volume growth and regulatory commentary. Beat = reinforce Tranche 3 add; miss or guidance cut = reassess position.
MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Deploy Tranche 3 ($242.41 post-CSPX). $200 CSPX unconditionally if no 5-7% S&P pullback; or $142.41 CSPX + $100 MA on pullback. No extensions.
ONGOING: ASML WHT verification — confirm 15% Dutch treaty or 30% US NRA rate on next IBKR tax document. If 30%, initiate switch to AMS-listed ASML shares.
MEDIUM-TERM: U03A stage-down from ~14% to ~10% of NAV as CSPX gap closes and Fed rate cuts materialize. Redeploy into CSPX.
